entities.name: PayPal
entities.how_described: 本集作为「PayPal 黑手党」的大本营被反复提及。因为 Peter Thiel 和 Max Levchin 调动了令人难以置信的人才密度，它不仅自身成功，还在随后的 25 年里裂变出大量优秀公司。被收购时约有 254 名员工，其中真正的「木桶」只有 12 到 17 个。

evidence.quote: He's part of the famous PayPal Mafia, where he was executive vice president of business development and policy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So, Vinod distilled it, but I actually learned this back on my PayPal days.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So in the early 2000s, why was PayPal so successful?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So it allowed PayPal to succeed where possibly we wouldn't have.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Back in the PayPal days, when I first started my career in technology, I actually truthfully was not very good at this.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So it was very difficult to get a job at PayPal, unless you had a first degree or second degree connection to the engineering team or to Peter through Stanford.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But what I learned to do is steal people from other people's organizations within PayPal very successfully.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So people that were in the building that I have lunch with or dinner with or go for a runaround the PayPal campus with, I was accurate at diagnosing their abilities.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So for example, at PayPal, we had about 254 people in Mountain View when we were acquired.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I learned this at PayPal.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Peter taught me this literally the first week of my job at PayPal, that the way to build a company, we were jogging around the Stanford campuses, you've got to find these undiscovered talent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Jeremy Stoppelman, he's worked with me very closely at PayPal before he started Yelp and got promoted to be engineering director and vice president of PayPal, had commercial instincts back when he was an individual contributor.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And six months in, so two board meetings in, he said to me, he's like, "I haven't seen this kind of tempo since our PayPal days."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: All these companies you invested in, all these businesses you've built, PayPal, all these things.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So you were famously part of the PayPal Mafia.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Square
entities.how_described: Keith 曾任该公司的首席运营官，且 Jack Dorsey 当时用 iPad 运营公司。本集以 Square 为例讲述了「木桶与弹药」框架的来源（冰沙测试），并提到红杉资本的 Roelof Botha 惊叹于其运营节奏。

evidence.quote: He's also been chief operating officer at Square, VP of corporate development at LinkedIn.

evidence.quote: So when I started working at Square, Jack Dorsey was running the company off an iPad.

evidence.quote: The most important lesson I learned when I was working at Square from my board was Vinod Khosla was on my board, and he said, "The team you build is the company you build."

evidence.quote: I've been a longtime investor from the seed round of a company called Faire, founded by two of my colleagues at Square, Max Rhodes and Jeff Colvison worked for me at Square and then the two other co-founders also worked at Square.

evidence.quote: They can be as simple as the now somewhat famous in technology smoothie test, which is, we used to have engineers work pretty hard at Square and pretty late.

entities.name: Ramp
entities.how_described: Keith 抢投了其 A 轮，因为看到他们仅用 3 个月就做完了大银行通常需要 9 到 12 个月才能完成的企业卡发布准备。该公司的每次董事会都以「自发布以来的天数」作为第一张幻灯片。

entities.name: Faire
entities.how_described: 由 Keith 在 Square 的两位同事创立。本集以投资人对其创始人 Max Rhodes 做背景调查时「问错问题 vs 问对问题」为例，探讨如何准确识别潜力，并提到其极其惊人的执行速度。

evidence.quote: I've been a longtime investor from the seed round of a company called Faire, founded by two of my colleagues at Square, Max Rhodes and Jeff Colvison worked for me at Square and then the two other co-founders also worked at Square.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so some venture capitalists, including some very good ones, were nervous about investing in Faire.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So the time between the seed and series A at Faire was pretty tight.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And Delian said to me after the second fare board meeting that he shadowed me at, he said, "If there's one company in Silicon Valley that would cause me to leave being the VC, it'd be Faire."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: DoorDash
entities.how_described: Keith 提到创始人 Tony 对每一位高级招聘都做 20 个背景调查，以此保证极高的招聘准确率；同时指出，他们创业时并不是通过问客户「需不需要送餐按钮」来验证市场的。

evidence.quote: He was an early investor in Stripe, Palantir, Airbnb, YouTube, DoorDash, Ramp, and dozens of other companies.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So for example, ruthless referencing to me means Tony at DoorDash does 20 references on every single senior hire, 20.

entities.name: Airbnb
entities.how_described: Keith 是其早期投资者，他认同 CEO Brian Chesky「把油门踩到底」的做法，认为公司越成功、CEO 越要无情地抵消组织的自满情绪。

evidence.quote: He was an early investor in Stripe, Palantir, Airbnb, YouTube, DoorDash, Ramp, and dozens of other companies.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So in your case, I would've asked you, "If you were CEO of Airbnb, what would you have done differently?"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Because I don't want them to criticize Airbnb.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then I have a follow-up question, which is usually gold, which is, let's say I asked you this question about Airbnb and you give me this great answer.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So Airbnb, you start with a contrarian take, but eventually if the company's going to succeed, it has to become consensus.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: To some extent, when Brian first pitched me on Airbnb, there was some interesting evidence he already had that this was going to be successful.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think Airbnb was a good example where he was solving his own problem, saw an opportunity.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 木桶与弹药 (barrels and ammunition)
entities.how_described: Keith 著名的管理与招聘框架：「木桶」指能独立把一个想法从零推到闭环成功的人（极为稀缺），「弹药」指执行所需的团队和资源。如果不增加木桶数量，只是一味地增加弹药，反而会徒增协作税让公司变慢。

evidence.quote: I want to talk about this framework that you have, barrels and ammunition, because this is really mind-expanding and helping people understand who to even hire.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 人才密度 (density of talent)
entities.how_described: 本集贯穿始终的底层逻辑。初创公司就像有工资帽的球队，只有调动极高的人才密度，依靠「未被发现的才华」（往往缺乏数据点的年轻人），才能在与大公司的竞争中获胜。

evidence.quote: It's because Peter Thiel and Max Levchin marshaled an incredible density of talent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Critical density of talent, you see companies just creating an unfair advantage.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: AI 时代的产品角色演进 (foundation models)
entities.file: AI 时代的产品角色演进
entities.how_described: 本集指出，由于基础模型能力以月为单位跃升，制定长达一年的路线图变得荒谬，传统 PM 角色将失去意义。未来属于具备商业嗅觉、能在本周发现机会下周就推出新功能的「超级个体」（既懂商业又懂构建）。

evidence.quote: Right now, the capabilities of foundation models or companies like Lovable and things like that are just so improving such a rapid rate that it makes no sense to have a year-long roadmap.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 心理安全感 (psychological safety)
entities.how_described: Keith 明确表示完全不相信这个概念。他认为高性能机器没有心理安全感，它们是为了赢，并推荐《Jordan Rules》来佐证：如果想成为 Michael Jordan，就得表现得像 Michael Jordan。

evidence.quote: High performance machines don't have psychological safety.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It feels like you're not focused on psychological safety as a core tenent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: High performance machines don't have psychological safety.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Do you feel like that's negatively correlated to this idea of psychological safety with success?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And if you overemphasize failures and people think they're going to get criticized, this is where psychological safety maybe has some validity, which is be ambitious, be bold.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
